channel.title: Just Another Blog
channel.description: Recent content on Just Another Blog

item.title: d-way Heap
item.description: Introduction Implementation Running time Miscellaneous Introduction First some basics. A heap is a complete binary tree, where each node satisfies a heap property: In a max-heap, heap property says that \( parent ≥ child \) . In a min-heap, heap property says that \( parent ≤ child \) . A complete tree is a binary tree where all levels, except possibly the last, are completely filled.

item.title: Mount exFAT external drive on boot
item.description: Find device UUID. $ blkid > /dev/sda1: LABEL="External 2T" UUID="40A1-F12E" TYPE="exfat" .... Add an entry in /etc/fstab for mounting this device on boot. UUID=40A1-F12E /mnt ext4 defaults 0 0 Finally confirm it that the changes are valid. $ sudo mount -a
